pages.title: AIP — Project Overview
pages.author: bob.miller
pages.content:
## Welcome to AIP!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **AIP**.

## Project Overview

AIP is an Apache open-source project focused on AI-powered project intelligence — aggregating data from multiple sources and generating reports automatically.
The project currently has approximately 196 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/aip.git
cd aip

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `ingestion-pipeline` | Core component handling ingestion-pipeline-related operations |
| `concern-engine` | Core component handling concern-engine-related operations |
| `report-agent` | Core component handling report-agent-related operations |
| `mcp-server` | Core component handling mcp-server-related operations |
| `storage` | Core component handling storage-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b aip-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/AIP

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@aip.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-aip
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-310: Ingestion-Pipeline Architecture Decision
pages.author: van.long
pages.content:
## Background

Module `ingestion-pipeline` in project **AIP** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: AIP-28.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `ingestion-pipeline` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: AIP-28
- ADR-246: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: AIP Report-Agent — Technical Design Document
pages.author: thu.hien
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `report-agent` in project **AIP**.
Epic tracking: AIP-28.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `report-agent` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[report-agent Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [AIP Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_report_agent_status_updated
    ON report_agent_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE report_agent_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
report-agent:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: AIP-43, AIP-77

pages.title: Runbook — AIP ingestion-pipeline
pages.author: bob.miller
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `ingestion-pipeline` in project **AIP**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [ingestion-pipeline-gateway] → [ingestion-pipeline-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh ingestion-pipeline

# 2. Start service
systemctl start aip-ingestion-pipeline

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### ingestion-pipeline fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u aip-ingestion-pipeline -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `aip-ingestion-pipeline --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM ingestion-pipeline_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@aip.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-aip@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `ingestion-pipeline_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `ingestion-pipeline_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `ingestion-pipeline_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `ingestion-pipeline_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: AIP 3.2.0 Release Notes
pages.author: sarah.lee
pages.content:
## AIP 3.2.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-03-14
**Release Manager**: peter.johnson

---

## Highlights

Version 3.2.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.1.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [AIP-5] Fix memory leak trong mcp-server serializer
- [AIP-20] Fix NullPointerException trong report-agent khi input rỗng
- [AIP-33] Fix memory leak trong concern-engine serializer
- [AIP-40] Correct storage timeout không được respect
- [AIP-46] Correct storage timeout không được respect

## Improvements

- [AIP-15] Cải thiện storage error message clarity
- [AIP-17] Thêm metrics cho mcp-server connection pool
- [AIP-45] Cải thiện report-agent throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [AIP-47] Cải thiện concern-engine error message clarity
- [AIP-48] Thêm metrics cho concern-engine connection pool

## Breaking Changes

- `concern-engine` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `aip.legacy.enabled` is no longer supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/aip.yml config/aip.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/aip.version>.*</aip.version>3.2.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-320.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với concern-engine when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.3.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 30 contributors for this release.

pages.title: AIP report-agent — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: grace.taylor
pages.content:
## FAQ — AIP / report-agent

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `report-agent`.

---

### Q: Why does `report-agent` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time aip-report-agent --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `report-agent` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export AIP_REPORT_AGENT_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.aip.report.agent" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
aip-cli set-log-level report-agent DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `report-agent_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `report-agent` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `aip.v2.*` sang `aip.*`
- Class `LegacyReportAgentClient` has been removed, use `ReportAgentClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run report-agent in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  report-agent:
    image: apache/aip-report-agent:latest
    environment:
      - AIP_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - AIP_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — AIP ingestion-pipeline Incident 2025-05
pages.author: iris.thomas
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-8634
- **Jira**: AIP-5
- **Severity**: P3
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-03T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +42 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +55 minutes
- **Affected service**: `ingestion-pipeline` in project AIP

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `ingestion-pipeline` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire AIP cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `ingestion-pipeline_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+8 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+20 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+32 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+70 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `ingestion-pipeline` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | sarah.lee | 2025-06-01 | AIP-5 |
| Add circuit breaker | maria.garcia | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | bao.chau | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | duc.anh | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: HADOOP — Project Overview
pages.author: lan.phuong
pages.content:
## Welcome to HADOOP!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **HADOOP**.

## Project Overview

HADOOP is an Apache open-source project focused on distributed storage and batch processing at petabyte scale.
The project currently has approximately 184 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/hadoop.git
cd hadoop

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `hdfs` | Core component handling hdfs-related operations |
| `yarn` | Core component handling yarn-related operations |
| `mapreduce` | Core component handling mapreduce-related operations |
| `common` | Core component handling common-related operations |
| `tools` | Core component handling tools-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b hadoop-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/HADOOP

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@hadoop.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-hadoop
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-478: Tools Architecture Decision
pages.author: duc.anh
pages.content:
## Background

Module `tools` in project **HADOOP** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: HADOOP-21.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `tools` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: HADOOP-21
- ADR-444: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: HADOOP Tools — Technical Design Document
pages.author: jack.jackson
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `tools` in project **HADOOP**.
Epic tracking: HADOOP-21.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `tools` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[tools Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [HADOOP Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_tools_status_updated
    ON tools_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE tools_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
tools:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: HADOOP-58, HADOOP-92

pages.title: Runbook — HADOOP hdfs
pages.author: iris.thomas
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `hdfs` in project **HADOOP**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [hdfs-gateway] → [hdfs-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh hdfs

# 2. Start service
systemctl start hadoop-hdfs

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### hdfs fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u hadoop-hdfs -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `hadoop-hdfs --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM hdfs_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@hadoop.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-hadoop@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `hdfs_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `hdfs_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `hdfs_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `hdfs_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: HADOOP 3.1.0 Release Notes
pages.author: alice.wang
pages.content:
## HADOOP 3.1.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-05-04
**Release Manager**: lan.phuong

---

## Highlights

Version 3.1.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.0.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [HADOOP-27] Sửa race condition trong yarn thread pool shutdown
- [HADOOP-50] Fix NullPointerException trong tools khi input rỗng
- [HADOOP-52] Fix memory leak trong common serializer
- [HADOOP-57] Fix NullPointerException trong yarn khi input rỗng
- [HADOOP-68] Fix memory leak trong mapreduce serializer

## Improvements

- [HADOOP-25] Cải thiện yarn throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [HADOOP-67] Cải thiện common error message clarity
- [HADOOP-72] Giảm tools startup latency 40%
- [HADOOP-85] Giảm common startup latency 40%
- [HADOOP-91] Cải thiện yarn throughput lên 2x qua batching

## Breaking Changes

- `mapreduce` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `hadoop.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/hadoop.yml config/hadoop.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/hadoop.version>.*</hadoop.version>3.1.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-310.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với common when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.2.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 24 contributors for this release.

pages.title: HADOOP hdfs — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: jack.jackson
pages.content:
## FAQ — HADOOP / hdfs

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `hdfs`.

---

### Q: Why does `hdfs` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time hadoop-hdfs --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `hdfs` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export HADOOP_HDFS_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.hadoop.hdfs" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
hadoop-cli set-log-level hdfs DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `hdfs_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `hdfs` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `hadoop.v2.*` sang `hadoop.*`
- Class `LegacyHdfsClient` has been removed, use `HdfsClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run hdfs in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  hdfs:
    image: apache/hadoop-hdfs:latest
    environment:
      - HADOOP_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - HADOOP_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — HADOOP common Incident 2025-05
pages.author: hoang.nam
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-6374
- **Jira**: HADOOP-27
- **Severity**: P3
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-05T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +41 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +86 minutes
- **Affected service**: `common` in project HADOOP

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `common` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire HADOOP cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `common_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+4 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+18 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+26 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+64 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `common` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | peter.johnson | 2025-06-01 | HADOOP-27 |
| Add circuit breaker | frank.moore | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | thi.nga | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | dan.davis | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: SPARK — Project Overview
pages.author: maria.garcia
pages.content:
## Welcome to SPARK!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **SPARK**.

## Project Overview

SPARK là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào in-memory distributed computing với unified analytics engine.
The project currently has approximately 95 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/spark.git
cd spark

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `core` | Core component handling core-related operations |
| `sql` | Core component handling sql-related operations |
| `streaming` | Core component handling streaming-related operations |
| `mllib` | Core component handling mllib-related operations |
| `graphx` | Core component handling graphx-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b spark-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/SPARK

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@spark.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-spark
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-751: Core Architecture Decision
pages.author: van.long
pages.content:
## Background

Module `core` in project **SPARK** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: SPARK-15.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `core` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: SPARK-15
- ADR-940: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: SPARK Sql — Technical Design Document
pages.author: lan.phuong
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `sql` in project **SPARK**.
Epic tracking: SPARK-15.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `sql` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[sql Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [SPARK Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_sql_status_updated
    ON sql_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE sql_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
sql:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: SPARK-21, SPARK-17

pages.title: Runbook — SPARK core
pages.author: thu.hien
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `core` in project **SPARK**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [core-gateway] → [core-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh core

# 2. Start service
systemctl start spark-core

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### core fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u spark-core -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `spark-core --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM core_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@spark.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-spark@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `core_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `core_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `core_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `core_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: SPARK 3.0.0 Release Notes
pages.author: dan.davis
pages.content:
## SPARK 3.0.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-05-05
**Release Manager**: xuan.mai

---

## Highlights

Version 3.0.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.0.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [SPARK-37] Fix NullPointerException trong graphx khi input rỗng
- [SPARK-45] Sửa race condition trong streaming thread pool shutdown
- [SPARK-64] Fix graphx trả về stale data sau failover
- [SPARK-67] Fix memory leak trong streaming serializer
- [SPARK-79] Fix memory leak trong graphx serializer

## Improvements

- [SPARK-24] Cải thiện sql error message clarity
- [SPARK-47] Cải thiện core throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [SPARK-49] Giảm mllib startup latency 40%
- [SPARK-66] Cải thiện graphx throughput lên 2x qua batching

## Breaking Changes

- `core` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `spark.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/spark.yml config/spark.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/spark.version>.*</spark.version>3.0.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-300.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với sql when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.1.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 37 contributors for this release.

pages.title: SPARK sql — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: xuan.mai
pages.content:
## FAQ — SPARK / sql

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `sql`.

---

### Q: Why does `sql` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time spark-sql --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `sql` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export SPARK_SQL_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.spark.sql" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
spark-cli set-log-level sql DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `sql_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `sql` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `spark.v2.*` sang `spark.*`
- Class `LegacySqlClient` has been removed, use `SqlClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run sql in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  sql:
    image: apache/spark-sql:latest
    environment:
      - SPARK_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - SPARK_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — SPARK core Incident 2025-04
pages.author: xuan.mai
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-8193
- **Jira**: SPARK-37
- **Severity**: P2
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-08T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +37 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +205 minutes
- **Affected service**: `core` in project SPARK

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `core` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire SPARK cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `core_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+3 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+12 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+34 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+60 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `core` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | grace.taylor | 2025-06-01 | SPARK-37 |
| Add circuit breaker | van.long | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | grace.taylor | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | thi.nga | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: KAFKA — Project Overview
pages.author: bao.chau
pages.content:
## Welcome to KAFKA!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **KAFKA**.

## Project Overview

KAFKA is an Apache open-source project focused on a distributed event streaming platform for real-time data pipelines.
The project currently has approximately 119 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/kafka.git
cd kafka

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `broker` | Core component handling broker-related operations |
| `consumer` | Core component handling consumer-related operations |
| `producer` | Core component handling producer-related operations |
| `streams` | Core component handling streams-related operations |
| `connect` | Core component handling connect-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b kafka-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/KAFKA

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@kafka.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-kafka
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-407: Consumer Architecture Decision
pages.author: maria.garcia
pages.content:
## Background

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `consumer` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: KAFKA-1

- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: KAFKA-16
- ADR-462: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: ADR-628: Streams Architecture Decision
pages.author: bob.miller
pages.content:
## Background

Module `streams` in project **KAFKA** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: KAFKA-23.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `streams` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: KAFKA-23
- ADR-474: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: KAFKA Consumer — Technical Design Document
pages.author: maria.garcia
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `consumer` in project **KAFKA**.
Epic tracking: KAFKA-1.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `consumer` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[consumer Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [KAFKA Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_consumer_status_updated
    ON consumer_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE consumer_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
consumer:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: KAFKA-46, KAFKA-40

pages.title: Runbook — KAFKA broker
pages.author: thi.nga
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `broker` in project **KAFKA**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [broker-gateway] → [broker-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh broker

# 2. Start service
systemctl start kafka-broker

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### broker fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u kafka-broker -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `kafka-broker --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM broker_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@kafka.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-kafka@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `broker_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `broker_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `broker_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `broker_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: KAFKA 3.0.0 Release Notes
pages.author: henry.anderson
pages.content:
## KAFKA 3.0.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-03-08
**Release Manager**: carol.brown

---

## Highlights

Version 3.0.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.0.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [KAFKA-6] Correct connect timeout không được respect
- [KAFKA-15] Fix streams trả về stale data sau failover
- [KAFKA-35] Correct streams timeout không được respect
- [KAFKA-36] Sửa race condition trong connect thread pool shutdown
- [KAFKA-41] Fix broker trả về stale data sau failover

## Improvements

- [KAFKA-11] Giảm producer startup latency 40%
- [KAFKA-27] Cải thiện connect throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [KAFKA-42] Tối ưu broker GC pressure
- [KAFKA-44] Cải thiện connect throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [KAFKA-52] Giảm broker startup latency 40%

## Breaking Changes

- `producer` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `kafka.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/kafka.yml config/kafka.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/kafka.version>.*</kafka.version>3.0.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-300.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với producer when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.1.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 30 contributors for this release.

pages.title: KAFKA producer — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: van.long
pages.content:
## FAQ — KAFKA / producer

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `producer`.

---

### Q: Why does `producer` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time kafka-producer --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `producer` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export KAFKA_PRODUCER_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.kafka.producer" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
kafka-cli set-log-level producer DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `producer_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `producer` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `kafka.v2.*` sang `kafka.*`
- Class `LegacyProducerClient` has been removed, use `ProducerClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run producer in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  producer:
    image: apache/kafka-producer:latest
    environment:
      - KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — KAFKA consumer Incident 2025-01
pages.author: henry.anderson
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-8398
- **Jira**: KAFKA-6
- **Severity**: P1
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-18T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +26 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +137 minutes
- **Affected service**: `consumer` in project KAFKA

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `consumer` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire KAFKA cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `consumer_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+2 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+12 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+21 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+79 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `consumer` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | carol.brown | 2025-06-01 | KAFKA-6 |
| Add circuit breaker | lan.phuong | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | bao.chau | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | peter.johnson | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: FLINK — Project Overview
pages.author: thi.nga
pages.content:
## Welcome to FLINK!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **FLINK**.

## Project Overview

FLINK là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào stateful computations over unbounded và bounded data streams.
The project currently has approximately 199 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/flink.git
cd flink

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `runtime` | Core component handling runtime-related operations |
| `streaming` | Core component handling streaming-related operations |
| `batch` | Core component handling batch-related operations |
| `table-api` | Core component handling table-api-related operations |
| `state-backend` | Core component handling state-backend-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b flink-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/FLINK

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@flink.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-flink
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-305: State-Backend Architecture Decision
pages.author: carol.brown
pages.content:
## Background

Module `state-backend` in project **FLINK** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: FLINK-15.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `state-backend` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: FLINK-15
- ADR-582: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: FLINK-42
- ADR-407: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: FLINK Table-Api — Technical Design Document
pages.author: thi.nga
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `table-api` in project **FLINK**.
Epic tracking: FLINK-15.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `table-api` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[table-api Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [FLINK Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_table_api_status_updated
    ON table_api_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE table_api_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
table-api:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: FLINK-42, FLINK-40

pages.title: Runbook — FLINK runtime
pages.author: sarah.lee
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `runtime` in project **FLINK**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [runtime-gateway] → [runtime-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh runtime

# 2. Start service
systemctl start flink-runtime

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### runtime fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u flink-runtime -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `flink-runtime --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM runtime_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@flink.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-flink@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `runtime_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `runtime_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `runtime_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `runtime_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: FLINK 3.0.0 Release Notes
pages.author: lan.phuong
pages.content:
## FLINK 3.0.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-04-12
**Release Manager**: minh.tuan

---

## Highlights

Version 3.0.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.0.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [FLINK-9] Fix NullPointerException trong batch khi input rỗng
- [FLINK-20] Fix NullPointerException trong runtime khi input rỗng
- [FLINK-51] Sửa race condition trong state-backend thread pool shutdown
- [FLINK-59] Fix runtime trả về stale data sau failover
- [FLINK-61] Correct streaming timeout không được respect

## Improvements

- [FLINK-5] Giảm table-api startup latency 40%
- [FLINK-18] Giảm table-api startup latency 40%
- [FLINK-35] Tối ưu runtime GC pressure
- [FLINK-39] Cải thiện streaming error message clarity
- [FLINK-49] Thêm metrics cho table-api connection pool

## Breaking Changes

- `streaming` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `flink.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/flink.yml config/flink.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/flink.version>.*</flink.version>3.0.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-300.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với batch when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.1.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 26 contributors for this release.

pages.title: FLINK table-api — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: quoc.hung
pages.content:
## FAQ — FLINK / table-api

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `table-api`.

---

### Q: Why does `table-api` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time flink-table-api --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `table-api` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export FLINK_TABLE_API_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.flink.table.api" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
flink-cli set-log-level table-api DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `table-api_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `table-api` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `flink.v2.*` sang `flink.*`
- Class `LegacyTableApiClient` has been removed, use `TableApiClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run table-api in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  table-api:
    image: apache/flink-table-api:latest
    environment:
      - FLINK_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - FLINK_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — FLINK table-api Incident 2025-01
pages.author: alice.wang
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-9865
- **Jira**: FLINK-9
- **Severity**: P3
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-01T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +11 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +61 minutes
- **Affected service**: `table-api` in project FLINK

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `table-api` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire FLINK cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `table-api_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+4 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+12 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+29 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+88 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `table-api` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | thu.hien | 2025-06-01 | FLINK-9 |
| Add circuit breaker | david.chen | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | xuan.mai | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | duc.anh | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: BEAM — Project Overview
pages.author: hoang.nam
pages.content:
## Welcome to BEAM!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **BEAM**.

## Project Overview

BEAM là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào unified programming model cho batch và streaming data processing.
The project currently has approximately 139 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/beam.git
cd beam

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `runners` | Core component handling runners-related operations |
| `sdk-java` | Core component handling sdk-java-related operations |
| `sdk-python` | Core component handling sdk-python-related operations |
| `transforms` | Core component handling transforms-related operations |
| `io` | Core component handling io-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b beam-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/BEAM

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@beam.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-beam
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-868: Transforms Architecture Decision
pages.author: maria.garcia
pages.content:
## Background

Module `transforms` in project **BEAM** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: BEAM-17.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `transforms` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: BEAM-17
- ADR-901: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: BEAM Runners — Technical Design Document
pages.author: bao.chau
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `runners` in project **BEAM**.
Epic tracking: BEAM-17.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `runners` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[runners Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [BEAM Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_runners_status_updated
    ON runners_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE runners_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
runners:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: BEAM-17, BEAM-46

## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `runners` in project **BEAM**.
Epic tracking: BEAM-24.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `runners` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[runners Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [BEAM Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_runners_status_updated
    ON runners_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE runners_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

runners:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50

  retry:

pages.title: Runbook — BEAM runners
pages.author: alice.wang
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `runners` in project **BEAM**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [runners-gateway] → [runners-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh runners

# 2. Start service
systemctl start beam-runners

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### runners fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u beam-runners -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `beam-runners --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM runners_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@beam.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-beam@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `runners_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `runners_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `runners_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `runners_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: BEAM 3.0.0 Release Notes
pages.author: frank.moore
pages.content:
## BEAM 3.0.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-05-15
**Release Manager**: maria.garcia

---

## Highlights

Version 3.0.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.0.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [BEAM-4] Fix NullPointerException trong transforms khi input rỗng
- [BEAM-11] Fix NullPointerException trong transforms khi input rỗng
- [BEAM-21] Fix sdk-python trả về stale data sau failover
- [BEAM-28] Fix io trả về stale data sau failover
- [BEAM-38] Sửa race condition trong io thread pool shutdown

## Improvements

- [BEAM-5] Cải thiện sdk-java throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [BEAM-6] Cải thiện transforms throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [BEAM-33] Cải thiện sdk-python error message clarity
- [BEAM-52] Cải thiện io throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [BEAM-62] Giảm sdk-java startup latency 40%

## Breaking Changes

- `sdk-java` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `beam.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/beam.yml config/beam.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/beam.version>.*</beam.version>3.0.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-300.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với transforms when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.1.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 34 contributors for this release.

pages.title: BEAM io — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: duc.anh
pages.content:
## FAQ — BEAM / io

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `io`.

---

### Q: Why does `io` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time beam-io --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `io` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export BEAM_IO_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.beam.io" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
beam-cli set-log-level io DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `io_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `io` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `beam.v2.*` sang `beam.*`
- Class `LegacyIoClient` has been removed, use `IoClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run io in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  io:
    image: apache/beam-io:latest
    environment:
      - BEAM_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - BEAM_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```
