pages.title: Postmortem — BEAM sdk-java Incident 2025-05
pages.author: maria.garcia
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-3363
- **Jira**: BEAM-4
- **Severity**: P2
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-09T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +11 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +184 minutes
- **Affected service**: `sdk-java` in project BEAM

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `sdk-java` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire BEAM cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `sdk-java_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+7 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+13 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+42 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+76 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `sdk-java` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | iris.thomas | 2025-06-01 | BEAM-4 |
| Add circuit breaker | xuan.mai | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | hoang.nam | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | duc.anh | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: HIVE — Project Overview
pages.author: minh.tuan
pages.content:
## Welcome to HIVE!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **HIVE**.

## Project Overview

HIVE là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào data warehouse infrastructure built on top of Apache Hadoop.
The project currently has approximately 133 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/hive.git
cd hive

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `metastore` | Core component handling metastore-related operations |
| `ql` | Core component handling ql-related operations |
| `serde` | Core component handling serde-related operations |
| `jdbc` | Core component handling jdbc-related operations |
| `cli` | Core component handling cli-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b hive-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/HIVE

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@hive.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-hive
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-860: Jdbc Architecture Decision
pages.author: thi.nga
pages.content:
## Background

Module `jdbc` in project **HIVE** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: HIVE-10.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `jdbc` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: HIVE-10
- ADR-741: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: HIVE Ql — Technical Design Document
pages.author: bob.miller
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `ql` in project **HIVE**.
Epic tracking: HIVE-10.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `ql` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[ql Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [HIVE Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_ql_status_updated
    ON ql_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE ql_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
ql:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: HIVE-37, HIVE-11

### In scope

- Refactor `ql` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[ql Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [HIVE Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_ql_status_updated
    ON ql_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE ql_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
ql:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: HIVE-11, HIVE-43

pages.title: Runbook — HIVE metastore
pages.author: thu.hien
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `metastore` in project **HIVE**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [metastore-gateway] → [metastore-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh metastore

# 2. Start service
systemctl start hive-metastore

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### metastore fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u hive-metastore -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `hive-metastore --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM metastore_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@hive.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-hive@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `metastore_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `metastore_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `metastore_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `metastore_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: HIVE 3.2.0 Release Notes
pages.author: peter.johnson
pages.content:
## HIVE 3.2.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-03-23
**Release Manager**: lan.phuong

---

## Highlights

Version 3.2.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.1.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [HIVE-48] Correct ql timeout không được respect
- [HIVE-52] Fix cli trả về stale data sau failover
- [HIVE-71] Sửa race condition trong serde thread pool shutdown
- [HIVE-78] Correct ql timeout không được respect
- [HIVE-80] Fix memory leak trong metastore serializer

## Improvements

- [HIVE-24] Tối ưu ql GC pressure
- [HIVE-25] Thêm metrics cho ql connection pool
- [HIVE-47] Thêm metrics cho serde connection pool
- [HIVE-65] Giảm metastore startup latency 40%
- [HIVE-92] Giảm serde startup latency 40%

## Breaking Changes

- `ql` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `hive.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/hive.yml config/hive.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/hive.version>.*</hive.version>3.2.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-320.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với jdbc when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.3.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 33 contributors for this release.

pages.title: HIVE ql — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: peter.johnson
pages.content:
## FAQ — HIVE / ql

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `ql`.

---

### Q: Why does `ql` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time hive-ql --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `ql` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export HIVE_QL_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.hive.ql" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
hive-cli set-log-level ql DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `ql_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `ql` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `hive.v2.*` sang `hive.*`
- Class `LegacyQlClient` has been removed, use `QlClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run ql in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  ql:
    image: apache/hive-ql:latest
    environment:
      - HIVE_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - HIVE_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — HIVE metastore Incident 2025-01
pages.author: xuan.mai
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-7514
- **Jira**: HIVE-48
- **Severity**: P2
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-22T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +23 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +88 minutes
- **Affected service**: `metastore` in project HIVE

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `metastore` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire HIVE cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `metastore_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+6 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+10 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+38 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+86 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `metastore` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | thu.hien | 2025-06-01 | HIVE-48 |
| Add circuit breaker | maria.garcia | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | frank.moore | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | henry.anderson | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: ZOOKEEPER — Project Overview
pages.author: bao.chau
pages.content:
## Welcome to ZOOKEEPER!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **ZOOKEEPER**.

## Project Overview

ZOOKEEPER là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào centralized service cho distributed coordination và configuration.
The project currently has approximately 60 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/zookeeper.git
cd zookeeper

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `server` | Core component handling server-related operations |
| `client` | Core component handling client-related operations |
| `admin` | Core component handling admin-related operations |
| `recipes` | Core component handling recipes-related operations |
| `security` | Core component handling security-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b zookeeper-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/ZOOKEEPER

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@zookeeper.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-zookeeper
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-165: Client Architecture Decision
pages.author: van.long
pages.content:
## Background

Module `client` in project **ZOOKEEPER** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: ZOOKEEPER-4.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `client` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: ZOOKEEPER-4
- ADR-621: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: ZOOKEEPER Client — Technical Design Document
pages.author: thi.nga
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `client` in project **ZOOKEEPER**.
Epic tracking: ZOOKEEPER-4.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `client` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[client Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [ZOOKEEPER Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_client_status_updated
    ON client_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE client_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
client:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: ZOOKEEPER-103, ZOOKEEPER-45

| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: ZOOKEEPER-4, ZOOKEEPER-34

pages.title: Runbook — ZOOKEEPER server
pages.author: minh.tuan
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `server` in project **ZOOKEEPER**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [server-gateway] → [server-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh server

# 2. Start service
systemctl start zookeeper-server

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### server fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u zookeeper-server -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `zookeeper-server --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM server_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@zookeeper.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-zookeeper@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `server_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `server_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `server_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `server_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: ZOOKEEPER 3.0.0 Release Notes
pages.author: van.long
pages.content:
## ZOOKEEPER 3.0.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-05-19
**Release Manager**: bao.chau

---

## Highlights

Version 3.0.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.0.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [ZOOKEEPER-3] Fix NullPointerException trong recipes khi input rỗng
- [ZOOKEEPER-10] Fix admin trả về stale data sau failover
- [ZOOKEEPER-22] Correct client timeout không được respect
- [ZOOKEEPER-40] Fix security trả về stale data sau failover
- [ZOOKEEPER-50] Fix admin trả về stale data sau failover

## Improvements

- [ZOOKEEPER-6] Thêm metrics cho server connection pool
- [ZOOKEEPER-7] Cải thiện client error message clarity
- [ZOOKEEPER-43] Cải thiện security error message clarity
- [ZOOKEEPER-52] Thêm metrics cho admin connection pool
- [ZOOKEEPER-64] Cải thiện security throughput lên 2x qua batching

## Breaking Changes

- `recipes` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `zookeeper.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/zookeeper.yml config/zookeeper.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/zookeeper.version>.*</zookeeper.version>3.0.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-300.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với security when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.1.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 33 contributors for this release.

pages.title: ZOOKEEPER client — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: eve.wilson
pages.content:
## FAQ — ZOOKEEPER / client

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `client`.

---

### Q: Why does `client` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time zookeeper-client --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `client` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export ZOOKEEPER_CLIENT_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.zookeeper.client" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
zookeeper-cli set-log-level client DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `client_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `client` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `zookeeper.v2.*` sang `zookeeper.*`
- Class `LegacyClientClient` has been removed, use `ClientClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run client in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  client:
    image: apache/zookeeper-client:latest
    environment:
      - ZOOKEEPER_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - ZOOKEEPER_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — ZOOKEEPER server Incident 2025-05
pages.author: carol.brown
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-1108
- **Jira**: ZOOKEEPER-3
- **Severity**: P1
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-07T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +25 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +133 minutes
- **Affected service**: `server` in project ZOOKEEPER

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `server` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire ZOOKEEPER cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `server_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+3 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+14 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+44 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+51 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `server` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | van.long | 2025-06-01 | ZOOKEEPER-3 |
| Add circuit breaker | hoang.nam | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | bob.miller | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | peter.johnson | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: CASSANDRA — Project Overview
pages.author: eve.wilson
pages.content:
## Welcome to CASSANDRA!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **CASSANDRA**.

## Project Overview

CASSANDRA là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào highly-scalable distributed NoSQL database.
The project currently has approximately 126 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/cassandra.git
cd cassandra

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `core` | Core component handling core-related operations |
| `cql` | Core component handling cql-related operations |
| `storage` | Core component handling storage-related operations |
| `gossip` | Core component handling gossip-related operations |
| `repair` | Core component handling repair-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b cassandra-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/CASSANDRA

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@cassandra.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-cassandra
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-433: Cql Architecture Decision
pages.author: carol.brown
pages.content:
## Background

Module `cql` in project **CASSANDRA** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: CASSANDRA-1.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `cql` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: CASSANDRA-1
- ADR-148: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: CASSANDRA Gossip — Technical Design Document
pages.author: peter.johnson
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `gossip` in project **CASSANDRA**.
Epic tracking: CASSANDRA-1.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `gossip` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[gossip Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [CASSANDRA Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_gossip_status_updated
    ON gossip_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE gossip_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
gossip:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: CASSANDRA-35, CASSANDRA-27

pages.title: Runbook — CASSANDRA core
pages.author: minh.tuan
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `core` in project **CASSANDRA**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [core-gateway] → [core-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh core

# 2. Start service
systemctl start cassandra-core

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### core fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u cassandra-core -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `cassandra-core --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM core_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@cassandra.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-cassandra@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `core_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `core_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `core_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `core_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: CASSANDRA 3.2.0 Release Notes
pages.author: david.chen
pages.content:
## CASSANDRA 3.2.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-04-04
**Release Manager**: thu.hien

---

## Highlights

Version 3.2.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.1.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [CASSANDRA-7] Correct core timeout không được respect
- [CASSANDRA-20] Fix core trả về stale data sau failover
- [CASSANDRA-26] Correct core timeout không được respect
- [CASSANDRA-28] Sửa race condition trong gossip thread pool shutdown
- [CASSANDRA-34] Fix NullPointerException trong core khi input rỗng

## Improvements

- [CASSANDRA-4] Cải thiện core throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [CASSANDRA-16] Cải thiện core throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [CASSANDRA-17] Cải thiện core throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [CASSANDRA-18] Cải thiện cql error message clarity
- [CASSANDRA-81] Giảm cql startup latency 40%

## Breaking Changes

- `cql` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `cassandra.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/cassandra.yml config/cassandra.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/cassandra.version>.*</cassandra.version>3.2.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-320.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với storage when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.3.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 29 contributors for this release.

pages.title: CASSANDRA repair — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: john.smith
pages.content:
## FAQ — CASSANDRA / repair

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `repair`.

---

### Q: Why does `repair` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time cassandra-repair --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `repair` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export CASSANDRA_REPAIR_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.cassandra.repair" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
cassandra-cli set-log-level repair DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `repair_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `repair` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `cassandra.v2.*` sang `cassandra.*`
- Class `LegacyRepairClient` has been removed, use `RepairClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run repair in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  repair:
    image: apache/cassandra-repair:latest
    environment:
      - CASSANDRA_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - CASSANDRA_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — CASSANDRA storage Incident 2025-02
pages.author: henry.anderson
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-2478
- **Jira**: CASSANDRA-7
- **Severity**: P3
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-10T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +23 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +51 minutes
- **Affected service**: `storage` in project CASSANDRA

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `storage` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire CASSANDRA cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `storage_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+3 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+14 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+20 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+76 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `storage` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | duc.anh | 2025-06-01 | CASSANDRA-7 |
| Add circuit breaker | grace.taylor | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | hoang.nam | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | bob.miller | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

pages.title: STORM — Project Overview
pages.author: bob.miller
pages.content:
## Welcome to STORM!

This document helps new developers quickly get familiar with the project **STORM**.

## Project Overview

STORM là một Apache open-source project tập trung vào distributed real-time computation system.
The project currently has approximately 136 active contributors worldwide.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

- JDK 17+ (recommended: Eclipse Temurin)
- Maven 3.8+ hoặc Gradle 8+
- Docker Desktop
- IntelliJ IDEA hoặc VS Code với Java extension

### Clone & Build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/apache/storm.git
cd storm

# Build the entire project (skip tests on first run)
mvn clean install -DskipTests

# Run tests
mvn test
```

### Local Development

```bash
# Start local stack
docker-compose -f dev/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Verify
curl http://localhost:9090/health
```

## Module Structure

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------|
| `core` | Core component handling core-related operations |
| `topology` | Core component handling topology-related operations |
| `spout` | Core component handling spout-related operations |
| `bolt` | Core component handling bolt-related operations |
| `nimbus` | Core component handling nimbus-related operations |

## Contribution Process

1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch: `git checkout -b storm-XXXX-description`
2. Write code + unit tests (coverage ≥ 80%)
3. Run `mvn checkstyle:check` and fix if needed
4. Create Pull Request, link with Jira ticket
5. Wait for at least 2 approvals from committers

## Jira Board

All tasks are tracked in Jira: https://issues.apache.org/jira/projects/STORM

- **Backlog**: Not yet assigned to a sprint
- **In Progress**: In progress
- **In Review**: Awaiting code review / approval
- **Done**: Done

## Contact

- **Mailing list**: dev@storm.apache.org
- **Slack**: #apache-storm
- **Office hours**: Every Wednesday, 15:00 UTC

pages.title: ADR-772: Spout Architecture Decision
pages.author: carol.brown
pages.content:
## Background

Module `spout` in project **STORM** needs to be redesigned to support horizontal scaling and improve processing latency. Corresponding epic: STORM-2.

## Decision

After evaluating the options, the team decided to:

1. Split `spout` into stateless worker nodes with a shared state store (Redis).
2. Apply event-driven architecture via an internal message bus.
3. Use the circuit breaker pattern for all external calls.

## Rationale

- **Scalability**: Stateless workers can scale horizontally without complex migrations.
- **Resilience**: Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when downstream services fail.
- **Observability**: Each worker emits its own metrics, easy to trace by request ID.

## Trade-offs

| Option | Advantages | Disadvantages |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Stateless + Redis | Easy to scale, independent deployment | Adds infrastructure dependency |
| Monolithic with sharding | Simpler | Bottleneck at shard boundaries |
| Actor model (Akka) | Built-in supervision | High learning curve, JVM overhead |

## Current Status

Proof-of-concept completed. Awaiting sign-off from the architecture review board before starting the implementation sprint.

## References

- Epic: STORM-2
- ADR-642: Previous decision on message bus selection
- RFC: Stateless worker proposal v2

pages.title: STORM Nimbus — Technical Design Document
pages.author: john.smith
pages.content:
## Overview

This document describes the technical design for refactoring the module `nimbus` in project **STORM**.
Epic tracking: STORM-2.

## Scope

### In scope

- Refactor `nimbus` core following Clean Architecture.
- Add observability: distributed tracing, structured logging.
- Improve performance: target P99 latency reduction of 40%.

### Out of scope

- UI/UX changes.
- Changes to the public API contract (backward compatibility must be maintained).

## Detailed Design

### Data Flow

```
Client Request
    │
    ▼
[API Gateway] ──── Auth ────▶ [Auth Service]
    │
    ▼
[nimbus Service]
    │           │
    ▼           ▼
[Cache]    [DB Layer]
              │
              ▼
        [STORM Storage]
```

### Schema changes

```sql
-- Add index to improve query performance
CREATE INDEX idx_nimbus_status_updated
    ON nimbus_events(status, updated_at);

-- Partition by month
ALTER TABLE nimbus_logs
    PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### New Configuration

```yaml
nimbus:
  pool:
    min-size: 5
    max-size: 50
    idle-timeout: 30s
  retry:
    max-attempts: 3
    backoff: exponential
    initial-interval: 100ms
  circuit-breaker:
    failure-threshold: 50%
    wait-duration: 30s
```

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|--------|---------|---------|-----------|
| Schema migration fails | Low | High | Blue-green deployment, rollback script ready |
| Performance regression | Medium | Medium | Load test in staging before deployment |
| API breaking change | Low | High | Contract testing with consumer-driven tests |

## Timeline

- **Week 1-2**: Schema design & review.
- **Week 3-4**: Implementation core logic.
- **Week 5**: Integration testing & performance benchmarking.
- **Week 6**: Staging deployment & sign-off.

## Dependencies

Blocking: STORM-2, STORM-22

pages.title: Runbook — STORM core
pages.author: maria.garcia
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `core` in project **STORM**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [core-gateway] → [core-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh core

# 2. Start service
systemctl start storm-core

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### core fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u storm-core -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `storm-core --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM core_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@storm.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-storm@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `core_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `core_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)

pages.title: Runbook — STORM topology
pages.author: jack.jackson
pages.content:
## Purpose

This runbook describes the operational procedures and troubleshooting for the module `topology` in project **STORM**.

## Architecture Overview

```
[Ingress] → [topology-gateway] → [topology-worker-pool] → [State Store]
                                                ↓
                                        [Metrics Sink]
```

## Startup Procedure

```bash
# 1. Check health of dependencies
./scripts/check-deps.sh topology

# 2. Start service
systemctl start storm-topology

# 3. Confirm service is healthy
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
```

## Common Troubleshooting

### topology fails to start

1. Check logs: `journalctl -u storm-topology -n 100`
2. Check port conflicts: `netstat -tlnp | grep 8080`
3. Verify config: `storm-topology --validate-config`

### High latency (> 500ms p99)

1. Check thread pool saturation: `/metrics/threadpool`
2. Check GC pressure: `jstat -gcutil <pid> 1000 5`
3. If GC overhead > 10%: restart with `-Xmx` increased by 50%

### Connection pool exhausted

```sql
SELECT count(*), state FROM topology_connections GROUP BY state;
```

If `waiting` > 80% of total: increase `pool.max-size` in `application.yml`.

## Escalation

| Severity | Response Time | Contact |
|----------|-------------------|---------|
| P1 (service down) | 15 minutes | oncall@storm.apache.org |
| P2 (degraded) | 1 hour | team-storm@apache.org |
| P3 (minor) | 1 day | JIRA ticket |

## Key Metrics to Monitor

- `topology_request_rate` — requests/sec
- `topology_error_rate` — errors/sec (alert if > 1%)
- `topology_p99_latency_ms` — alert if > 500ms
- `topology_queue_depth` — alert if > 10000

pages.title: STORM 3.2.0 Release Notes
pages.author: grace.taylor
pages.content:
## STORM 3.2.0 Release Notes

**Release Date**: 2025-03-17
**Release Manager**: xuan.mai

---

## Highlights

Version 3.2.0 focuses on **stability**, **performance**, and **security hardening**.
This is the most significant release since 3.1.0.

## Bug Fixes

- [STORM-26] Sửa race condition trong spout thread pool shutdown
- [STORM-44] Sửa race condition trong core thread pool shutdown
- [STORM-64] Correct bolt timeout không được respect
- [STORM-65] Fix bolt trả về stale data sau failover
- [STORM-77] Correct bolt timeout không được respect

## Improvements

- [STORM-7] Thêm metrics cho core connection pool
- [STORM-9] Cải thiện core throughput lên 2x qua batching
- [STORM-10] Tối ưu core GC pressure
- [STORM-19] Tối ưu nimbus GC pressure
- [STORM-34] Tối ưu spout GC pressure

## Breaking Changes

- `topology` API: method `processLegacy()` has been removed. Use `process()` instead.
- Config key `storm.legacy.enabled` không còn supported.

## Upgrade Guide

```bash
# 1. Backup configuration
cp config/storm.yml config/storm.yml.bak

# 2. Update version in pom.xml
sed -i 's/storm.version>.*</storm.version>3.2.0</g' pom.xml

# 3. Run migration script
./scripts/migrate-320.sh

# 4. Verify
mvn test -Dtest=MigrationVerificationTest
```

## Known Issues

- Issue với nimbus when running on ARM64 (fix expected in 3.3.0).
- Memory usage higher than expected under heavy write workload — workaround: reduce `batch.size`.

## Contributors

Thanks to 36 contributors for this release.

pages.title: STORM topology — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: henry.anderson
pages.content:
## FAQ — STORM / topology

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `topology`.

---

### Q: Why does `topology` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time storm-topology --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `topology` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export STORM_TOPOLOGY_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.storm.topology" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
storm-cli set-log-level topology DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `topology_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `topology` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `storm.v2.*` sang `storm.*`
- Class `LegacyTopologyClient` has been removed, use `TopologyClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run topology in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  topology:
    image: apache/storm-topology:latest
    environment:
      - STORM_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - STORM_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: STORM nimbus — FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.author: peter.johnson
pages.content:
## FAQ — STORM / nimbus

This page collects frequently asked questions and solutions for the module `nimbus`.

---

### Q: Why does `nimbus` starts slowly (> 30s)?

**A**: Usually due to slow DNS lookup or connection timeout to external dependency.
Check with:
```bash
time storm-nimbus --dry-run
```
If most of the time is DNS, add an entry to `/etc/hosts` or configure a local DNS cache.

---

### Q: `nimbus` reports `OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space`

**A**: Increase heap size:
```bash
export STORM_NIMBUS_OPTS="-Xms512m -Xmx4g"
```
If still OOM, check for memory leak using heap dump:
```bash
jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof <pid>
```

---

### Q: How to enable debug logging?

**A**: Trong `log4j2.xml`:
```xml
<Logger name="org.apache.storm.nimbus" level="DEBUG"/>
```
Hoặc qua JMX runtime:
```bash
storm-cli set-log-level nimbus DEBUG
```

---

### Q: Metric `nimbus_lag` keeps increasing, what is the cause?

**A**: Consumer lag increasing may be due to:
1. Consumer processing too slow — profile bottleneck in business logic.
2. Producer throughput spike — check producer metrics.
3. `nimbus` is being throttled — check quota configuration.

---

### Q: Upgrading from v2.x lên v3.x have any breaking changes?

**A**: See details in Migration Guide. Main breaking changes:
- Config namespace changed from `storm.v2.*` sang `storm.*`
- Class `LegacyNimbusClient` has been removed, use `NimbusClient`
- Default port changed from 9090 to 9191

---

### Q: How to run nimbus in Docker?

```yaml
services:
  nimbus:
    image: apache/storm-nimbus:latest
    environment:
      - STORM_CLUSTER_ID=local-dev
      - STORM_ZOOKEEPER=zookeeper:2181
    ports:
      - "9191:9191"
    depends_on:
      - zookeeper
```

pages.title: Postmortem — STORM nimbus Incident 2025-03
pages.author: xuan.mai
pages.content:
## Incident Information

- **Incident ID**: INC-8156
- **Jira**: STORM-26
- **Severity**: P1
- **Time bắt đầu**: 2025-05-18T00:00:00.000+0000
- **Time phát hiện**: +32 minutes
- **Time khắc phục**: +184 minutes
- **Affected service**: `nimbus` in project STORM

## Summary

The incident occurred when the module `nimbus` could not handle the sudden traffic spike,
leading to connection pool exhaustion and cascading timeouts across the entire STORM cluster.

## Timeline

| Time | Event |
|-----------|---------|
| T+0 | Alerting fires: `nimbus_error_rate > 5%` |
| T+4 min | On-call engineer received alert |
| T+17 min | Root cause identified: connection pool exhausted |
| T+33 min | Hotfix deployed: pool size increased from 20 → 100 |
| T+53 min | Service fully recovered |

## Root Cause Analysis

**Immediate cause**: The connection pool of `nimbus` reached the `max-size=20` limit
while traffic increased 5x from baseline.

**Contributing factors**:
1. Pool size was not reviewed when scaling traffic up.
2. No circuit breaker to automatically degrade gracefully.
3. Alert threshold too high (5% error rate to trigger, should be 1%).

**5 Whys**:
1. Why does service down? → Connection pool exhausted.
2. Why does pool exhausted? → Pool size quá nhỏ cho current traffic.
3. Why does không resize? → Capacity review không được thực hiện định kỳ.
4. Why does không có capacity review? → Chưa có process chuẩn.
5. Why does chưa có process? → Technical debt item chưa được prioritize.

## Action Items

| Task | Owner | Deadline | Jira |
|------|-------|---------|------|
| Increase default pool size | john.smith | 2025-06-01 | STORM-26 |
| Add circuit breaker | xuan.mai | 2025-06-05 | TBD |
| Update alert threshold to 1% | hoang.nam | 2025-05-31 | TBD |
| Capacity review process | grace.taylor | 2025-06-12 | TBD |

## Lessons Learned

- **Detection**: Alert threshold needs review — 5% is too high, user impact occurs earlier.
- **Response**: Runbook is missing the step to check connection pool → add to runbook.
- **Prevention**: Capacity planning must be part of the release process.

- **CASSANDRA-88** ([STALLED] Support multi-tenant cql configuration): assigned to peter.johnson, last update 2025-05-15, vẫn đang In Progress. Cần follow-up.
- **CASSANDRA-22** ([STALLED] Replace deprecated repair API calls): assigned to henry.anderson, last update 2025-05-14, vẫn đang In Progress. Cần follow-up.

### 🚧 Blockers tồn tại

- **CASSANDRA-15**: [BLOCKER] Add dark mode to core dashboard — đang chặn các task downstream.
- **CASSANDRA-40**: [BLOCKER] Document core configuration options — đang chặn các task downstream.

## What went well

- CI/CD pipeline ổn định, không có flaky tests.
- Daily standup ngắn gọn và focused.
- Code review turnaround time trung bình < 24h.

## What needs improvement

- Một số task bị stall do thiếu context — cần cải thiện ticket description.
- Dependency giữa các teams cần được track rõ hơn qua Jira link.
- Estimation accuracy cần cải thiện (actual vs estimate lệch 26%).

## Action items

1. Tech Lead review tất cả In-Progress tickets > 3 day vào mỗi thứ Tư.
2. Tạo checklist template cho ticket description chuẩn.
3. Tổ chức refinement session 2 lần/sprint thay vì 1 lần.

pages.title: AIP Team Meeting — 2025-05-28 (Anomaly Injection: Cross-source Conflict)
pages.author: alice.wang
pages.content:
## AIP Team Meeting — 2025-05-28

**Date**: 2025-05-28
**Project**: AIP
**Facilitator**: alice.wang
**Attendees**: hoang.nam, van.long, minh.tuan, alice.wang

---

## Agenda

1. Sprint progress review
2. Cross-source status verification
3. Deadline risk assessment
4. Blocker resolution

---

## Status Update — AIP-30

Van Long confirmed that **AIP-30** (Update report-agent dependencies to latest stable) is **still pending review**. The dependency bump PR has been submitted but is awaiting code review approval and has NOT been merged yet. The task is currently **chưa xong** (not yet complete) — the Jira ticket should remain open until the PR is merged and validated in staging.

> ⚠️ Note: If Jira shows this ticket as Done or Resolved, that status is **incorrect**. The actual state is still in_progress / pending merge.

## Deadline Risk — AIP-53

**AIP-53** (Allow ingestion-pipeline to auto-scale replicas) is due **2025-05-30** — tomorrow. The feature is still in progress. Alice Wang flagged this as a critical deadline risk. Hoang Nam needs to provide an update by EOD today.

## Action Items

- **AIP-30** (@van.long): Dependency update is still pending review / chờ review — do NOT mark as Done until PR is merged. Due: 2025-05-30.
- **AIP-53** (@hoang.nam): Auto-scale replicas feature. Status: in_progress. Due: 2025-05-30 (DEADLINE RISK).

_ground_truth.description: CONF-INJ-001 explicitly states AIP-30 is still pending/chưa xong while Jira marks it Done. Also flags AIP-53 as a deadline risk (due 2025-05-30).

pages.title: AIP Sprint Review Notes — 2025-05-28 (Anomaly Injection: Stalled + Blocker)
pages.author: hoang.nam
pages.content:
## AIP Sprint Review — 2025-05-28

**Date**: 2025-05-28
**Project**: AIP
**Facilitator**: hoang.nam
**Attendees**: hoang.nam, van.long, minh.tuan, alice.wang

---

## Stalled Task — AIP-37

**AIP-37** (Write concern-engine migration script) was raised as a concern. Minh Tuan confirmed that **no progress has been made since 2025-05-19** — over 10 days without any update. The task is **đang bị đình trệ** (stalled). The team requires an update by EOD 2025-05-29 or the task will be escalated to the PM.

**Last known state (from 2025-05-19):** The migration script skeleton was created but test coverage is at 0%. No commits since that date.

## Blocker — AIP-67 blocking AIP-14

**AIP-67** (Upgrade concern-engine to use Netty 4.2) is a **critical blocker** for **AIP-14** (Upgrade ingestion-pipeline to use Netty 4.2).

This blocker has been open for **5 days** (since 2025-05-24) with no resolution. The infrastructure team has not yet provided the required compatibility matrix for Netty 4.2 with the current JDK 17 setup.

> **Escalation required:** If AIP-67 is not resolved by 2025-05-30, both AIP-67 and AIP-14 will miss their next milestone window.

## Action Items

- **AIP-37** (@minh.tuan): Provide status update by EOD 2025-05-29. Task has been stalled / đình trệ since 2025-05-19.
- **AIP-67** (@van.long): Resolve Netty compatibility blocker. Blocking AIP-14 for 5+ days. Escalate to infra team immediately.
- **AIP-14** (@van.long): Blocked until AIP-67 is resolved.

_ground_truth.description: CONF-INJ-002 documents AIP-37 as stalled (no update since 2025-05-19, 10 days) and AIP-67 as a blocker for AIP-14 (open 5 days).
